steps.label: Initial Report Details Entry
steps.description: On this screen, you will enter the initial details for a new child welfare intake report. You will see fields for the date and time of the report, the type of report, urgency, and a brief summary. Guidance is provided to help you accurately capture the essential information needed to initiate the intake process. Please review all instructions and ensure all required fields are completed before proceeding. This information is critical for establishing the context and urgency of the intake.

fields.label: Report Date
fields.error_message: Report date and time is required.

data_validation: Report date cannot be in the future.
Report type must be selected.
Urgency level must be selected.
Summary must not exceed 1000 characters.
Intake worker name and ID must match logged-in user.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to the next step after entering all required report details.

buttons.label: Cancel
buttons.description: Cancel the intake process and return to the dashboard.

ACTION_1.name: Trigger Prior History Search
ACTION_1.implementation: On 'Next' button, triggers the system to query agency database for prior history and transitions to STEP_2.
ACTION_1.triggerCondition: Intake Worker clicks 'Next' and all required fields are validated.

steps.label: Search Prior History
steps.description: Upon 'Search Prior History' action, the system queries the agency database for any prior reports, cases, or patterns of involvement related to the child or household. The system must handle errors such as database transaction closure and display appropriate error messages to the intake worker.

rules.label: Database Query Execution
rules.logic: When the intake worker initiates a search, the system must execute a query against the agency database for prior involvement records. If the query fails due to a closed transaction, the system must return an error status and message to the user.

rules.label: Error Handling
rules.logic: If the database returns an error with status 'error' and a message indicating transaction closure, the system must display the error message to the intake worker and prevent further processing until resolved.

ACTION_2.name: Navigate to Intake Data Entry
ACTION_2.implementation: After successful prior history search, navigates to STEP_3 for intake data entry and documentation.

steps.label: Intake Data Entry and Documentation
steps.description: This ScreenQueue guides the Intake Worker through the complete intake process, including child and household member information, allegation documentation, reporter details, and additional narrative/context. Each screen ensures accurate, validated, and comprehensive data collection for intake processing.

sequenceLogic.skipLogic: Screens may be skipped if user is not eligible, workflow is cancelled, or auto_approval_enabled is true.

quick_action_buttons.label: Print
quick_action_buttons.description: Prints the entire intake summary for review or record keeping. Excludes attachments.

quick_action_buttons.label: Export PDF
quick_action_buttons.description: Exports the full intake data and documentation as a PDF file for download or sharing.

screens.label: Child and Household Member Information Entry
screens.description: On this screen, you will enter detailed information about the child and all household members associated with the intake. Guidance is provided for each field to ensure accurate and complete data entry. You can add multiple household members, specifying their relationship to the child. Please review instructions for each section and ensure all required fields are completed. If you need to add more individuals, use the 'Add Household Member' button. The system will help you avoid duplicate entries and ensure relationships are correctly recorded.

fields.label: Race/Ethnicity
options: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

fields.label: Date of Birth
fields.error_message: Date of birth must be valid.

data_validation: Cannot have more than one mother or father per child.
Birth date cannot be in the future.
SSN must be unique for each individual.
Cannot have duplicate household member entries.
If relationship is 'Guardian', age must be at least 18.
If relationship is 'Sibling', age must be less than or equal to child age plus 20 years.

buttons.label: Add Household Member
buttons.description: Click to add another household member to the list.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to the next step after entering all required details.

header_buttons.label: Home
header_buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard home screen.

header_buttons.label: Back
header_buttons.description: Go back to the previous screen in the intake workflow.

header_buttons.label: Help
header_buttons.description: Open help documentation for intake data entry.

header_buttons.label: Settings
header_buttons.description: Access user and intake workflow settings.

header_buttons.label: User Profile
header_buttons.description: View and edit your user profile.

footer_buttons.label: Next
footer_buttons.description: Proceed to the next screen after validating all required child and household member details.

workflow_steps.description: Validate all required fields for child and household members.
Navigate to the next screen in the intake workflow.

business_rules.rule_description: SSN must be unique for each individual.
Guardian must be at least 18 years old.
business_rules.error_message: Guardian must be at least 18 years old.

disable_conditions.disable_reason: Complete all required fields to proceed.

cross_field_validation.error_message: Cannot have more than one mother or father per child.

data_format_rules.format_description: SSN must be in valid format.

ACTION_1.name: Open Help Modal
ACTION_1.implementation: Opens help documentation for intake data entry.

ACTION_2.name: Validate Child and Household Data
ACTION_2.implementation: Validates all required fields and business rules for child and household members.

ACTION_3.name: Navigate to Next Screen
ACTION_3.implementation: Moves user to the next screen in the intake workflow.

screens.label: Allegation Documentation
screens.description: This screen allows you to document all allegations related to the intake. For each allegation, specify the type, alleged victim, alleged perpetrator, and a brief description. You may add multiple allegations as needed. Instructions are provided to help you select the correct allegation type and ensure all required information is captured. Please review each entry for accuracy before proceeding.
data_validation: Allegation type must be selected for each entry.
Alleged victim and perpetrator cannot be the same individual.
Date of alleged incident cannot be in the future.
Cannot have duplicate allegations for the same victim and perpetrator.
Allegation description must be provided for each entry.

buttons.label: Add Allegation
buttons.description: Click to add another allegation to the list.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to the next step after entering all allegations.

header_buttons.label: Home
header_buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard home screen.

header_buttons.label: Back
header_buttons.description: Go back to the previous screen in the intake workflow.

header_buttons.label: Help
header_buttons.description: Open help documentation for allegation documentation.

header_buttons.label: Settings
header_buttons.description: Access user and intake workflow settings.

header_buttons.label: User Profile
header_buttons.description: View and edit your user profile.

footer_buttons.label: Next
footer_buttons.description: Proceed to the next screen after validating all allegations.

workflow_steps.description: Validate all required fields for allegations.
Navigate to the next screen in the intake workflow.

success_message: Moved to Reporter Information Entry screen.

business_rules.rule_description: Alleged victim and perpetrator cannot be the same individual.
business_rules.error_message: Victim and perpetrator cannot be the same.

disable_conditions.disable_reason: Complete all required fields to proceed.

cross_field_validation.error_message: Duplicate allegation detected for same victim and perpetrator.

data_format_rules.format_description: Date must be valid and not in the future.

ACTION_4.name: Open Help Modal
ACTION_4.implementation: Opens help documentation for allegation documentation.

ACTION_5.name: Validate Allegation Data
ACTION_5.implementation: Validates all required fields and business rules for allegations.

ACTION_6.name: Navigate to Next Screen
ACTION_6.implementation: Moves user to the next screen in the intake workflow.

screens.label: Reporter Information Entry
screens.description: On this screen, enter the details of the person reporting the incident. This includes their name, contact information, relationship to the child, and whether they wish to remain anonymous. Instructions are provided to help you complete each field accurately. If the reporter is anonymous, only minimal information is required. Please ensure all required fields are completed before proceeding.

fields.label: Reporter Full Name
fields.error_message: Reporter name is required unless anonymous.

fields.label: Anonymous
fields.error_message: Select if reporter wishes to remain anonymous.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.error_message: Phone number must be valid format.

fields.label: Email Address
fields.error_message: Email address must be valid format.

data_validation: If anonymous is selected, name, phone, and address are not required.
Phone number must be valid format if provided.
Email address must be valid format if provided.
Cannot have duplicate reporter entries.
If relationship is 'Parent', name must be provided.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to the next step after entering reporter information.

required_fields.error_message: Reporter name is required unless anonymous.

business_rules.rule_description: If relationship is 'Parent', name must be provided.

disable_conditions.disable_reason: Complete all required fields to proceed.

data_format_rules.format_description: Phone number must be valid format.
Email address must be valid format.

ACTION_7.implementation: Opens help documentation for reporter information entry.

ACTION_8.name: Validate Reporter Data
ACTION_8.implementation: Validates all required fields and business rules for reporter information.

ACTION_9.name: Navigate to Next Screen
ACTION_9.implementation: Moves user to the next screen in the intake workflow.

config.confirm_message: Moved to Additional Narrative and Context.

screens.label: Additional Narrative and Context
screens.description: This screen provides a space for you to enter any additional narrative or contextual details relevant to the intake. Use the text area to describe circumstances, observations, or other information that may not fit into previous sections. Guidance is provided to help you include all pertinent details. Please review your entry for completeness and clarity before submitting.
data_validation: Narrative description must be provided.
Narrative should not contain prohibited or offensive language.
Narrative must be at least 20 characters.

buttons.label: Submit
buttons.description: Submit the intake narrative and proceed to disposition.

header_buttons.label: Home
header_buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard home screen.

header_buttons.label: Back

footer_buttons.label: Submit
footer_buttons.description: Submit the intake narrative and complete the intake process.

workflow_steps.description: Validate narrative description for completeness and prohibited language.

error_message: Narrative must be at least 20 characters.

workflow_steps.description: Submit the intake narrative and finalize the intake workflow.

business_rules.rule_description: Narrative must be at least 20 characters.
business_rules.error_message: Narrative must be at least 20 characters.

business_rules.rule_description: Narrative should not contain prohibited or offensive language.
business_rules.error_message: Narrative contains prohibited or offensive language.

disable_conditions.disable_reason: Complete all required fields to proceed.

ACTION_11.implementation: Validates narrative description for completeness and prohibited language.

ACTION_12.name: Submit Intake
ACTION_12.implementation: Submits the intake narrative and finalizes the intake workflow.

ACTION_3.name: Trigger Intake Submission Validation
ACTION_3.implementation: On completion of all intake screens, triggers validation of all required fields and logical consistency before routing to disposition (STEP_4).
ACTION_3.triggerCondition: All intake screens completed and required fields validated.

steps.label: Submit Intake for Disposition
steps.description: When the intake worker submits the completed intake for disposition, the system must validate all required fields, check date formats and logical consistency, and route the intake to the disposition decision node. Any errors encountered during submission, such as database transaction issues, must be reported to the user.

rules.label: Intake Submission Validation
rules.logic: On submit, the system must validate that all required fields are completed and that all date fields are in correct format and logically consistent. If validation fails, the system must return an error status and message.

rules.label: Error Handling on Submission
rules.logic: If a database error occurs during submission (e.g., transaction closed), the system must return the error status and message, and prevent routing to the disposition node until resolved.

ACTION_4.name: Mark Intake Workflow Complete
ACTION_4.implementation: Upon successful validation and submission, marks the intake workflow as complete and routes to disposition decision (STEP_5).
ACTION_4.triggerCondition: Intake submission validation passes and no critical errors present.

steps.label: Disposition Decision
steps.description: The intake worker reviews all intake information and selects a disposition: 'screen in' (meets criteria for investigation/assessment) or 'screen out' (does not meet criteria). The rationale field is required. Any errors encountered during this process, such as database transaction issues, must be reported to the user.

rules.label: Disposition Selection
rules.logic: The intake worker must select either 'screen in' or 'screen out' and provide a rationale. The system must validate that the rationale field is completed before allowing submission.

rules.label: Error Handling on Disposition
rules.logic: If a database error occurs during disposition selection (e.g., transaction closed), the system must return the error status and message, and prevent finalization of the disposition until resolved.

ACTION_5.name: Return to Initial Report Entry
ACTION_5.implementation: On 'Return' button, navigates back to STEP_1 for corrections or re-entry.
ACTION_5.triggerCondition: Intake Worker clicks 'Return' during disposition decision.

steps.label: Assign to Supervisor
steps.description: Process to assign the completed intake record to the supervisor for review and approval. The system must route all entered intake data, including audit trail, to the supervisor's queue. The assignment should ensure that the intake record is complete and ready for supervisor action, and that all relevant data is transferred securely and accurately.

assignments.label: Assign Intake Record to Supervisor Queue
assignments.logic: Upon completion of intake and disposition decision, the system validates the intake record status. If no critical errors (e.g., database transaction errors) are present, the intake record, along with its audit trail, is routed to the supervisor's review queue. If a system error such as 'Unexpected Neo4j error: The result is out of scope. The associated transaction has been closed.' is detected in the status or message fields, the assignment process must halt and notify the intake worker to resolve the error before proceeding.

ACTION_6.name: Assign Intake Record to Supervisor
ACTION_6.implementation: Routes the completed intake record and audit trail to the supervisor's queue for review and approval.
ACTION_6.triggerCondition: Intake record is complete, disposition decision made, and no critical errors present.

steps.label: Review Intake Assignment
steps.description: Supervisor reviews the assigned intake record for approval, ensuring all entered data and the audit trail are complete and accurate. Supervisor must verify that all required fields are filled, data is logically consistent, and the audit trail reflects all actions taken. Supervisor must either approve the intake or return it for corrections, providing comments as needed.

completionConditions.logic: Supervisor must review all intake record details, confirm completeness and accuracy, verify audit trail, and record an approval or return decision with comments. Task is complete when supervisor submits approval or returns for correction.
